slides.title: Muscle is the organ of *longevity*.
slides.sub: This isn't about looking jacked. It's about how well you age, and almost nobody frames it this way.

slides.title: Muscle is your glucose sink
slides.body: Muscle is where you store and burn blood sugar. More muscle means *steadier blood sugar* across the day.

slides.title: You lose it with age
slides.body: Muscle quietly fades with age, leading to *falls and frailty* later. Strength training can reverse much of that.

slides.title: Feed it through the day
slides.body: Muscle is built from protein, so spread *protein across your meals* instead of cramming it into one.

slides.title: Start with two sessions
slides.body: *Two strength sessions a week* is enough to start building and protecting muscle. You don't need a gym.

slides.title: Load life, not just weights
slides.body: Carry the groceries, climb the stairs, squat to the floor. *Everyday loading counts* toward staying strong.

slides.title: Build strength for the *long game*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your training and movement and rolls it into your daily score, so the work you put in is visible on the trend line.

caption:
Muscle is the organ of longevity, and it's about how you age, not looking jacked. 💪

It's where you store blood sugar, you lose it with age, and two strength sessions a week starts rebuilding it. Carry, climb, squat to the floor. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health tracks your training and rolls it into your daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Muscle Is the Organ of Longevity
